raw_consumer_statement: Around XXXX of XXXX, my wife made a small purchase at XXXX XXXX XXXX  that I was unaware of, including two other purchases from XXXX  XXXX & XXXX XXXX. I was not responsible for any of the purchases ; however, Macy  's XXXX XXXX ) C/C had placed me on " paperless '' w/o my consent or acknowledgement. Macy 's subsequently charged me for a {$30.00} late fee. When I first contacted Macy 's, I was dispatched overseas. It was hard to understand their broken English, and they usually told you one thing and got the opposite result. At first, they reversed the {$30.00} late fee and opened an investigation for fraudulent purchases. However, I later learned that my wife used the C/C for one purchase at XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Macy 's could never give me a straight answer, but in a nutshell, I had been told that I was originally charged back {$30.00} as a result of XXXX XXXX only crediting {$30.00} of a {$45.00} charge, which is unheard of. Who only receives back a portion of a charge in dispute? The XXXX XXXX had been reversed and remains reversed at present. I had been advised by my wife, that she only made the one single purchase from XXXX. However, Macy 's allocated that they did not charge back the {$30.00} late fee, but had charged me {$15.00} for a remaining balance due, and then threatened me with additional late fees & interest, supposedly reversing the additional {$30.00} plus interest and forcing me to pay them an additional {$30.00}, which apparently a reversal late fee charge and allocating that I still owed them for a purchase we NEVER made! I then filed a dispute with Macy 's around late XX/XX/XXXX and was told that I need not be concerned about any payment and that they would have someone call me back within a few days. I received NO call back or response, other than more late fees! I also did not receive my XX/XX/XXXX bill, despite my demanding for monthly paper statements. Macy 's therefore is responsible to reimburse me for the loss and NOT harm my credit in any way! Yet, I'm still receiving interest & late fee letters from them, in addition to being shown against my online account. I had no choice but to pay them for an additional {$30.00} on XX/XX/XXXX. For the record, my wife does not shop at XXXX XXXX and hasn't purchased anything from XXXX XXXX since XXXX. I believe Macy 's is well aware of inside theft & fraud, as like XXXX, and they're concealing the truth and capitalizing on their losses. Macy 's is acting in a fraudulent manner. Please assist. Thank you!
